Off Calendar May-25-2017 Continued To Aug-22-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CARLA CAMARGO, TEL. NO. 1-415-699-1714, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Milton Miltiadous' application for the pro hac vice admission of Joseph Perkins is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/hk

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CARLA CAMARGO, TEL. NO. 1-415-699-1714, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO OPPOSING PARTY TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. Defendant Milton Miltiadous' motion to quash service of process is granted. Plaintiff Carla Camago failed to present evidence establishing proper service on Mr. Miltiadous or that he has sufficient minimum contacts with California warranting the imposition of specific personal jurisdiction over him. The majority view is that, per Article 10 of the Hague Convention, Japan's Central Authority is required to receive the summons and complaint and a Japanese court official must serve them. (Honda Motor Co. v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal. App. 4th 1043). The authorities relied on by Ms. Camago are distinguishable. The alleged defamatory postings that occurred in Japan are insufficient to support personal jurisdiction per Burdick v. Superior Court (2015) 233 Cal. App. 4th 8. Burdick is controlling because there is no evidence that Mr. Miltiadous specifically directed his conduct at California. The use of a gmail account is too attenuated for the imposition of specific jurisdiction. Ms. Camago's request for a continuance to take jurisdictional discovery is denied because this motion is granted on the alternative ground of improper service and she fails to explain how the discovery would enable her to support jurisdiction over Mr. Miltiadous. Order signed.
Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/hk
